label: Find a Name
description: Deciding on a name — or living with uncertainty about one — is its own process. There is no required order of operations, and no timeline. This item is a place to start if you want to begin exploring.

process.summary: Name exploration is personal. Some people know immediately; others try several names over time before one fits. Online name databases, community spaces, and trusted people in your life are useful starting points.

steps.description: Browse name databases to find names that feel worth trying. Nameberry, Behind the Name, and similar sites let you filter by origin, sound, and meaning.
Try a name on privately — write it, say it, sign it — and notice how it feels. There is no commitment involved.
Try it with a trusted person first, then broaden from there if it feels right.

sources.label: Behind the Name — name meanings and origins
